2606.30372.title: Using Large Language Models as Low-Cost Statistical Estimators for Human-Response Data
2606.30372.paper_summary: Quantitative research across the social and behavioral sciences depends on human subject experiments that are expensive, slow, and subject to sampling bias. Here we show that pretrained large language models induce risk-equivalent estimators of conditional expectations under squared loss, establishing restricted functional risk equivalence: under squared loss, the LLM induces an estimator whose risk matches the Bayes optimal risk for squared-loss prediction of conditional expectations for any inference that depends on the data only through the conditional mean. We formalize the LLM as a misspecified functional estimator $T(\hat{P}_n)$ trained on i.i.d.\ data, decompose the estimation error into representation bias $ε_{\mathrm{rep}}$ and optimization error, and prove that under mild regularity conditions the LLM's expected error converges to the irreducible population variance plus the squared representation bias, with the representation bias bounded by the Pinsker inequality. The identifiability error $δ$ propagates into the effective bias, inflating the asymptotic risk floor. We establish restricted functional risk equivalence via a bidirectional Le Cam deficiency analysis: the forward deficiency vanishes asymptotically while the reverse deficiency is exactly zero. We provide finite-sample concentration bounds and a calibration protocol with explicit decision rules. The result is a precise, provable statement: a well-calibrated LLM achieves the Bayes-optimal risk for conditional-mean-dependent inference, bounded by explicit scope conditions. In practical applications, this means that under satisfied conditions and well-calibrated models, large language models can be used in many prediction and decision-making tasks that originally relied on human experiments, approximating near-optimal statistical inference at lower cost.
2606.30372.paper_summary_zh: 定量研究在社會和行為科學中依賴於昂貴、緩慢且易受抽樣偏差影響的人類受試者實驗。<lb/>在這裡，我們展示了預訓練的大型語言模型在平方損失下引入了風險等價的條件期望估計器，建立了限制性功能風險等價性：在平方損失下，LLM引入了一個估計器，其風險與任何僅依賴於條件均值的條件期望平方損失預測的貝葉斯最優風險相匹配。<lb/>我們將LLM形式化為一個在獨立同分佈數據上訓練的錯誤規範功能估計器 $T(\hat{P}_n)$，將估計誤差分解為表示偏差 $ε_{\mathrm{rep}}$ 和優化誤差，並證明在溫和的正則條件下，LLM的期望誤差收斂於不可約的母體方差加上平方表示偏差，表示偏差受到Pinsker不等式的限制。<lb/>可識別性誤差 $δ$ 會傳播到有效偏差中，膨脹漸近風險底線。<lb/>我們通過雙向Le Cam缺陷分析建立了限制性功能風險等價性：前向缺陷漸近消失，而反向缺陷恰好為零。<lb/>我們提供有限樣本集中界限和具有明確決策規則的校準協議。<lb/>結果是一個精確的、可證明的陳述：一個良好校準的LLM實現了條件均值依賴推斷的貝葉斯最優風險，並受到明確範圍條件的限制。<lb/>在實際應用中，這意味著在滿足條件和良好校準的模型下，大型語言模型可以用於許多最初依賴於人類實驗的預測和決策任務，以較低的成本近似最佳的統計推斷。
2606.30372.author: Haobo Yang et.al.
